titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/197

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=447 status=live modified=2017-03-30 13:59:30.024428+00 | translation moa=448 status=live modified=2017-03-30 13:59:32.360277+00

div.p:
Omnibus sancte Matris ecclesie filiis Rogerus Auenel filius Geruasii Auenel Salutem . Sciatis me concessisse et hac carta mea confirmasse deo et ecclesie Sancte Marie
de Melros et Monachis ibidem deo seruientibus donationem Roberti auenel aui mei . quam Geruasius auenel pater meus eis confirmauit . scilicet terram de eskedale quam
predictus Robertus auenel auus meus illis donauit in perpetuam elemosinam annuente piissimo Rege Malcolmo prius . et postea illustri Rege Willelmo fratre eius . scilicet
Tumlouher et Watkerroc in liberam . et puram . et perpetuam elemosinam . pro anima gloriosi Regis dauid qui eandem terram donauerat eidem [auo] meo pro seruicio suo et pro anima
comitis Henrici et predicti Malcolmi Regis . et pro anima dicti Willelmi Regis et Alexandri filii Regis scottorum domini mei . et omnium antecessorum et successorum eius . et pro anima mea . et
omnium antecessorum et successorum meorum . Hanc itaque scilicet Tumlouher et Watkerroc concessi predictis Monachis et dedi et confirmaui et hac carta mea incartaui . per has diuisas . Vi
delicet sicut duo eskes conueniunt . et ita sursum per blac eske usque ad primum torrentem qui de Hertesfel descendit . et de illo torrente sursum per medium condos montium inter ipsos
et Robertum de brus sicut aque descendunt uersus ipsos Monachos . et ita inter forestam de timei et ipsos per medium condos montium sicut aque descendunt uersus ipsos . similiter inter ter
ram de borthwic et ipsos Monachos per medium condos montium sicut aque descendunt uersus ipsos . et ita usque ad terram de hawic . et inde in transuersum per medium condos
montium que est inter meipsum et predictos Monachos sicut aque descendunt uersus ipsos . que condos includit Harwde uersus Monachos . et ita se extendit ex orientali parte
de ipso harwde . et sic semper descendendo usque ubi duo eskes conueniunt . Totam autem hanc prenominatam terram et possessionem et quicquid continetur infra has predictas
diuisas in bosco et plano . in pratis et pasturis . in aquis et mariscis . et in omnibus utilitatibus et aisiamentis concessi et dedi et confirmaui antedictis Monachis in puram et perpetuam
elemosinam perpetuo iure tenendam et possidendam . liberam et quietam a me et heredibus meis et soluta ab omnibus seruitiis . et ab omni consuetudine et exaccione seculari . sicut liberius
et quietius plenius et honorificentius aliqua elemosina teneri et possideri potest . Et siquis forte aliquando hanc predictam terram calumniari uoluerit  ego et heredes mei omni calum
nianti respondebimus et sufficienter defendemus eam predictis monachis et warantizabimus . Hoc autem sciendum est que mihi et heredibus meis retineo tantum . ceruum et ceruam . porcum et
capreolum infra prenominatas diuisas  absque damno tamen et lesione segetum . pratorum . sepium . pecudum . animalium et omnium aliarum possessionum eorundem Monachorum . Ita tamen
quod ego et heredes mei forestarium non habebimus infra predicta diuisas . verumptamen habebimus infra easdem diuisas latrones et accipitrum et sperueriorum nidos et custodes
ad illos custodiendos et forisueias . Et sciendum quod monachi predicti habebunt et possidebunt totam predictam terram liberam et quietam a me et heredibus meis salua predicta
saluagina mea et nidis accipitrum et sperueriorum . et ipsi Monachi utentur omni hoc tenemento et facient logias ubicumque uoluerint pro libitu et uoluntate sua sicut melius et utilius
sibi et fratribus suis seruientibus et aueriis suis proficere uisum fuerit . Excepto quod non uenabuntur ibi cum motis et cordis . nec alios ibi ducent ad uenandum . nec pedicas ibi ponent nisi ad
capiendos lupos . neque accipeient infra has diuisas accipitrum et sperueriorum nidos . Preterea sciendum quod quinque marchas quas aliquando auus meus in firmam accepit ab eis . qua . . .
uxor eius necnon et pater meus illis ante mortem suam quietas clamauerit . has ego similiter quietas clamaui et eis in elemosinam dedi et in perpetuum remisi et hoc idem presenti car
ta confirmaui . Et ut hec elemosina stabilis et firma sit in perpetuum nec ullomodo possit in irritum duci dominus meus Willelmus Rex scottorum peticione et rogatu
eandem elemosinam eis concessit et dedit . et carta sua confirmauit . Testibus Ric’ Abbate de kelco . Waltero olif’ . Willelmo filio iohannis . Iohanne de Maccuswel . Bernardo de haud
Rogero burnard . Gregorio de ruthirforde . Willelmo de cresseuil . Willelmo persona fratre meo . et aliis Multis .

To all sons of holy Mother Church, Roger Avenel, son of Gervase Avenel, sends greeting. Know me to have granted and by this my charter to have confirmed to God and the church of Saint Mary at Melrose, and to the Monks serving God in that place, the donation of Robert Avenel, my grandfather, which Gervase Avenel, my father, confirmed to them; that is, the land of Eskdale which the aforesiad Robert Avenel, my grandfather, donated to them in everlasting alms, the most pious King Malcolm first approving, and afterwards the illustrious King William, his brother; to wit, Tomleuchar and Watcarrick, in free, pure, and everlasting alms; for the soul of the glorious King David, who had donated the same land to my same [grandfather] [in copying from the earlier charter, the scribe originally wrote 'patri', but erased it, forgetting to insert 'auo'] in return for his service; and for the soul of Earl Henry and of the aforesaid King Malcolm, and for the soul of the said King William and of Alexander, the son of the king of Scots, my lord, and of all his ancestors' and successors' [souls], and for my own soul and of all my ancestors' and successors' [souls]. I have therefore granted this, to wit, Tomleuchar and Watcarrik, to the aforesaid monks, and have given and confirmed and described in a written deed by this my charter, by these boundaries, namely, as the two Esks meet, so upwards by the Black Esk as far as the first stream that descends from Hartside; and from that stream, upwards through the middle ridge of the mountains, between them and Robert de Brus, as the waters descend towards the monks themselves; and so between the forest of Timeus and themselves, by the middle ridge of the mountains, as they descend towards themselves; similarly between the land of Borthwick and the monks themselves, by the middle ridge of the mountains, as the waters descend towards them; and so as far as the land of Hawick, and thence across by the middle ridge of the mountains which is between myself and the aforesaid monks, as the waters descend towards them; this ridge encloses Harwde towards the monks, and so descends from the east side from Harwde itself, and so always descending to where the two Esks meet.And this whole aforenamed land, possession, and whatever is contained within these aforesaid boundaries, in wood and plain, in meadows and pastures, in waters and marshes, and in all benefits and easements, I have granted and given, and made firm to the aforesaid monks, in pure and everlasting alms, to be held and possessed by perpetual right, free and quit from me and my heirs, and released from all services, and from every custom and secular exaction, as freely and quitely, fully and honourably, as any alms is able to be held and possessed. And if by chance anyone should wish to claim the aforesaid alms, I and my heirs shall answer all claimants, and sufficiently protect and warrant it for the aforesaid monks. This is also to be known: that I have retained for myself and my heirs only deer and hind, swine and roebuck, within the aforenamed boundaries, without harm and injury of crops, meadows, hedges, livestock, animals, and all the other possessions of the same monks. And although I and my heirs shall not have a forester within the described boundaries, nevertheless we shall have within the same boundaries robbers, and eyries of hawks and sparrow-hawks, and the guards to guard them, and trespassers; and it should be known that the aforesaid monks shall have and possess the whole aforesaid land, free and quit from me and my heirs, saving my aforesaid wild game, and the eyries of hawks and sparrow-hawks; and the monks shall make use of all this tenement according to their will and pleasure, just as it shall seem to them best and most useful to be of use to themselves, their brethren, their servants and livestock. With the exception that they shall not hunt there with packs of hounds and ropes, nor bring others there for hunting, nor set traps there, unless for catching wolves; and they shall not take the eyries of hawks and sparrow-hawks within these boundaries. It should be known in addition that the five merks which at one time my grandfather received in ferme from them, which ... his wife and also my father quitclaimed to them before his death; this I likewise have quitclaimed and given to them in alms and have remitted for ever, and this same ... I have confirmed by charter. And that this alms should be established and firm for ever, nor could by any means be declared invalid, the lord William, king of Scots, by [my] petition and request, has granted and given [this] alms to them, and confirmed by his charter. As witnesses, Richard, abbot of Kelso, Walter Oliphant, William son of John, John of Maxwell, Bernard of Howden, Roger Burnard, Gregory of Rutherford, William of Cresswell, William the parson, my brother, and many others.
